items.headline: Do Rising Earnings Estimates Reframe Albemarle's (ALB) Lithium Strategy as Discipline or Cyclicality?
items.snippet: In recent weeks, Albemarle has attracted heightened attention as analysts lifted earnings estimates for the current quarter and fiscal years, ahead of its August 5, 2026 earnings release.

items.headline: Will ALB's Expansion Actions Power Stronger Sales Volumes?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation ALB is strategically executing its projects aimed at boosting its global lithium conversion capacity. ALB saw higher sales volumes (up 14% year over year) in its Energy Storage unit in the first quarter.

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month, underperforming the Zacks Chemical - Diversified industry and the S&P 500's declines of 6.7% and 0.1%, respectively. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately.

items.headline: Albemarle and Cars.com have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
items.snippet: The demand backdrop is roaring. Energy Storage Systems GWh production jumped 117% year over year in the quarter. Management affirmed its FY26 outlook of $5.7 billion to $6.0 billion in revenue.

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB) broadens the lens from rare earths to critical minerals generally. It's the world's largest lithium producer. First-quarter 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% year over year to $664 million as lithium prices rebounded.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: The price of lithium is bouncing back. After lithium prices collapsed from their 2022 peaks, the market finally found its floor in late 2024 and has staged a resilient year-to-date rebound. Chinese spot prices have climbed back into the $23 per kilogram (kg) range.
